claim: The carrier density in Fe₁₋ₓCuₓSe increases significantly in the high-pressure phase above 10 kbar compared to the low-pressure nematic phase.
The superconducting coherence length in LaRh₃B₂ is approximately 60 nm at 0 K and 114 nm near the transition temperature.

X-ray diffraction patterns of La₀.₇Sr₀.₃MnO₃/Sr₃Al₂O₆ films grown on pristine and recycled SrTiO₃ substrates show the (008) Sr₃Al₂O₆ peak on the left side and the (002) La₀.₇Sr₀.₃MnO₃ peak on the right side of the (002) SrTiO₃ peak, with no detectable impurity phases in the 20-80° range.

The total volume fraction of particles in the cold-rolled and non-isothermally annealed Al-Mn alloy increases from approximately 1% to 5% during recovery and recrystallization, with constituent particles showing a significant increase in volume fraction.
The maximum zero-bias conductance of the Kondo peak in the NbSe₂-based quantum dot system reaches 25 μS (0.32 e²/h) at the base temperature of 28 mK.
The electrostatic potential drop across graphene-electrolyte interfaces ranges from -0.984 V to -1.09 V across different electrolyte types and concentrations.
The average lattice parameter of Ti_x(HfNbTaZr)_(1-x)/4 alloys in the bcc phase decreases linearly from 3.436 Å to 3.252 Å as Ti content increases from x = 0 to x = 1.0

Introducing one Ge atom defect into the Au-fcc(011)/Ge(001) variant D interface reduces its interfacial energy from 0.437 J/m² to 0.343 J/m², making it energetically comparable to the optimal variant C configuration.
SrCoO₃ thin films undergo a metal-to-insulator transition at a tensile strain threshold of approximately 1.0%

Among structural-compositional descriptors, the vacancy formation energy of mobile ions, crystal polarizability, and symmetry of the perfect lattice show strong correlations with ion diffusivity in solid-state electrolytes, with Spearman correlation coefficients of -52%, +55%, and +57%, respectively.
